heading: Officers, staff, and other personnel

Permitting Current Executive Director, Deputy Executive Directors, and General Counsel of Office of Compliance To Serve One Additional Term, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–38, § 1119 Stat. 408July 27, 2005“(a).—Executive DirectorNotwithstanding section 302(a)(3) of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 (), the individual serving as Executive Director of the Office of Compliance [now Office of Congressional Workplace Rights] as of the date of the enactment of this Act [] may serve one additional term.2 U.S.C. 1382(a)(3)July 27, 2005“(b).—Deputy Executive DirectorsNotwithstanding section 302(b)(2) of such Act (), any individual serving as a Deputy Executive Director of the Office of Compliance [now Office of Congressional Workplace Rights] as of the date of the enactment of this Act may serve one additional term.2 U.S.C. 1382(b)(2)“(c).—General CounselNotwithstanding section 302(c)(5) of such Act (), the individual serving as General Counsel of the Office of Compliance [now Office of Congressional Workplace Rights] as of the date of the enactment of this Act may serve one additional term.”2 U.S.C. 1382(c)(5)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Chair, subject to the approval of the Board, shall appoint and may remove an Executive Director. Selection and appointment of the Executive Director shall be without regard to political affiliation and solely on the basis of fitness to perform the duties of the Office. The first Executive Director shall be appointed no later than 90 days after the initial appointment of the Board of Directors.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: B Qualifications
children.text: The Executive Director shall be an individual with training or expertise in the application of laws referred to in .section 1302(a) of this title

children.heading: Disqualifications
children.label: C Disqualifications
children.text: The disqualifications in shall apply to the appointment of the Executive Director.section 1381(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Authority to fix compensation
children.label: A Authority to fix compensation
children.text: The Chair may fix the compensation of the Executive Director.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The rate of pay for the Executive Director may not exceed the maximum rate of pay in effect under .section 4575(f) of this title

children.heading: Term
children.label: 3 Term
children.text: The term of office of the Executive Director shall be not more than 2 terms of 5 years, except that the first Executive Director shall have a single term of 7 years.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 4 Duties
children.text: The Executive Director shall serve as the chief operating officer of the Office. Except as otherwise specified in this chapter, the Executive Director shall carry out all of the responsibilities of the Office under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Chair, subject to the approval of the Board, shall appoint and may remove a Deputy Executive Director for the Senate and a Deputy Executive Director for the House of Representatives. Selection and appointment of a Deputy Executive Director shall be without regard to political affiliation and solely on the basis of fitness to perform the duties of the office. The disqualifications in shall apply to the appointment of a Deputy Executive Director.section 1381(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Term
children.label: 2 Term
children.text: The term of office of a Deputy Executive Director shall be not more than 2 terms of 5 years, except that the first Deputy Executive Directors shall have a single term of 6 years.

children.heading: Authority to fix compensation
children.label: A Authority to fix compensation
children.text: The Chair may fix the compensation of the Deputy Executive Directors.

children.label: i
children.text: the highest annual rate of compensation of any officer of the Senate; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the highest annual rate of compensation of any officer of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 4 Duties
children.text: The Deputy Executive Director for the Senate shall recommend to the Board regulations under , maintain the regulations and all records pertaining to the regulations, and shall assume such other responsibilities as may be delegated by the Executive Director. The Deputy Executive Director for the House of Representatives shall recommend to the Board the regulations under , maintain the regulations and all records pertaining to the regulations, and shall assume such other responsibilities as may be delegated by the Executive Director.section 1384(a)(2)(B)(i) of this titlesection 1384(a)(2)(B)(ii) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Chair, subject to the approval of the Board, shall appoint a General Counsel. Selection and appointment of the General Counsel shall be without regard to political affiliation and solely on the basis of fitness to perform the duties of the Office. The disqualifications in shall apply to the appointment of a General Counsel.section 1381(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Authority to fix compensation
children.label: A Authority to fix compensation
children.text: The Chair may fix the compensation of the General Counsel.

children.label: i
children.text: the highest annual rate of compensation of any officer of the Senate; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the highest annual rate of compensation of any officer of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: exercise the authorities and perform the duties of the General Counsel as specified in this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise assist the Board and the Executive Director in carrying out their duties and powers, including representing the Office in any judicial proceeding under this chapter.

children.heading: Attorneys in the office of the General Counsel
children.label: 4 Attorneys in the office of the General Counsel
children.text: The General Counsel shall appoint, and fix the compensation of, and may remove, such additional attorneys as may be necessary to enable the General Counsel to perform the General Counsel’s duties.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 5 Term
children.text: The term of office of the General Counsel shall be not more than 2 terms of 5 years.

children.label: i
children.text: disability that substantially prevents the General Counsel from carrying out the duties of the General Counsel,

children.label: ii
children.text: incompetence,

children.label: iii
children.text: neglect of duty,

children.label: iv
children.text: malfeasance, including a felony or conduct involving moral turpitude, or

children.label: v
children.text: holding an office or employment or engaging in an activity that disqualifies the individual from service as the General Counsel under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Statement of reasons for removal
children.label: B Statement of reasons for removal
children.text: In removing the General Counsel, the Speaker of the House of Representatives and the President pro tempore of the Senate shall state in writing to the General Counsel the specific reasons for the removal.

children.label: A
children.text: appoint, and fix the compensation of, and may remove, 1 or more confidential advisors to carry out the duties described in this subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: designate 1 or more employees of the Office to serve as a confidential advisor.

children.heading: Voluntary services
children.label: A Voluntary services
children.text: A confidential advisor appointed or designated under paragraph (1) shall offer to provide to covered employees described in paragraph (4) the services described in subparagraph (B), which a covered employee may accept or decline.

children.label: i
children.text: informing, on a privileged and confidential basis, a covered employee who has been subject to a practice that may be a violation of part A of subchapter II about the employee’s rights under this chapter;

children.label: I
children.text: the roles, responsibilities, and authority of the Office; and

children.label: II
children.text: the relative merits of securing private counsel, designating a non-attorney representative, or proceeding without representation for proceedings before the Office;

children.label: iii
children.text: advising and consulting with, on a privileged and confidential basis, a covered employee who has been subject to a practice that may be a violation of part A of subchapter II regarding any claims the covered employee may have under subchapter IV, the factual allegations that support each such claim, and the relative merits of the procedural options available to the employee for each such claim;

children.label: I
children.text: assisting or consulting with the covered employee regarding the drafting of a claim to be filed under ; andsection 1402(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: consulting with the covered employee regarding the procedural options available to the covered employee after a claim is filed, and the relative merits of each option; and

children.label: v
children.text: informing, on a privileged and confidential basis, a covered employee who has been subject to a practice that may be a violation of part A of subchapter II about the option of pursuing, in appropriate circumstances, a complaint with the Committee on Ethics of the House of Representatives or the Select Committee on Ethics of the Senate.

children.heading: Continuity of service
children.label: C Continuity of service
children.text: Once a covered employee has accepted and received any services offered under this section from a confidential advisor appointed or designated under paragraph (1), any other services requested under this subsection by the covered employee shall be provided, to the extent practicable, by the same confidential advisor.

children.label: A
children.text: is admitted to practice before, and is in good standing with, the bar of a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, or a territory of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: has experience representing clients in cases involving the workplace laws incorporated by part A of subchapter II.

children.label: A
children.text: a former covered employee may only request such services if the practice that may be a violation of part A of subchapter II occurred during the employment or service of the employee; and

children.label: B
children.text: a covered employee described in this paragraph may only request such services before the expiration of the 180-day period described in .section 1402(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall not act as the designated representative for any covered employee in connection with the covered employee’s participation in any proceeding, including any proceeding under this chapter, any judicial proceeding, or any proceeding before any committee of Congress;

children.label: B
children.text: shall not offer or provide services described in paragraph (2)(B) to a covered employee if the covered employee has designated an attorney representative in connection with the covered employee’s participation in any proceeding under this chapter, except that a confidential advisor may provide general assistance and information to such attorney representative regarding this chapter and the role of the Office as the confidential advisor determines appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall not serve as a mediator in any mediation conducted pursuant to .section 1403 of this title

children.heading: Other staff
children.label: e Other staff
children.text: The Executive Director shall appoint, and fix the compensation of, and may remove, such other additional staff, including hearing officers, but not including attorneys employed in the office of the General Counsel, as may be necessary to enable the Office to perform its duties.

children.heading: Detailed personnel
children.label: f Detailed personnel
children.text: The Executive Director may, with the prior consent of the department or agency of the Federal Government concerned, use on a reimbursable or nonreimbursable basis the services of personnel of any such department or agency, including the services of members or personnel of the Government Accountability Office Personnel Appeals Board.

children.heading: Consultants
children.label: g Consultants
children.text: In carrying out the functions of the Office, the Executive Director may procure the temporary (not to exceed 1 year) or intermittent services of consultants.
